children.name: Meliphaga
children.description: Meliphaga — a genus of honeyeaters in the family Meliphagidae.

children.name: Meliphaga aruensis
children.description: The puff-backed honeyeater is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is widely spread throughout New Guinea. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Meliphaga chrysocephala
children.description: Meliphaga chrysocephala — a species in the genus Meliphaga, family Meliphagidae. Also known as honeyeaters.

children.name: Meliphaga chrysotis
children.description: Meliphaga chrysotis — a species in the genus Meliphaga, family Meliphagidae. Also known as honeyeaters.

children.name: Meliphaga lewinii
children.description: Lewin's honeyeater is a bird that inhabits the ranges along the east coast of Australia. It has a semicircular ear-patch, pale yellow in colour.

children.name: Meliphaga maculata
children.description: Meliphaga maculata — a species in the genus Meliphaga, family Meliphagidae. Also known as honeyeaters.

children.name: Meliphaga notata
children.description: The yellow-spotted honeyeater is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is also known as the lesser lewin. The bird is endemic to northern Queensland.

children.name: Meliarchus
children.description: Meliarchus — a genus of honeyeaters in the family Meliphagidae.

children.name: Meliarchus sclateri
children.description: The Makira honeyeater, also known as the San Cristobal honeyeater, is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to Makira in the Solomon Islands.

children.name: Oreornis chrysogenys
children.description: The orange-cheeked honeyeater is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It was formerly the only species placed in the genus Oreornis. It is endemic to West Papua, Indonesia.

children.name: Plectorhyncha
children.description: Plectorhyncha — a genus of honeyeaters in the family Meliphagidae.

children.name: Plectorhyncha lanceolata
children.description: The striped honeyeater is a passerine bird of the honeyeater family, Meliphagidae, found in Australia. It is a medium-sized honeyeater, about 23 cm (9.1 in) in length. Both sexes are a light greyish brown with dark brown centres to the feathers, which give the appearance of stripes.

children.name: Myzomela
children.description: Myzomela — a genus of honeyeaters in the family Meliphagidae.

children.name: Myzomela blasii
children.description: The drab myzomela or Seram myzomela is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to the islands of Seram, Boano and Ambon in Indonesia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Myzomela cineracea
children.description: The ashy myzomela is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is found in New Britain and Umboi Island in Papua New Guinea. It was previously considered a subspecies of the ruby-throated myzomela.

children.name: Myzomela cruentata
children.description: The red myzomela is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is found in New Guinea and New Britain. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Myzomela eichhorni
children.description: The crimson-rumped myzomela or yellow-vented myzomela, is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to the Western Province.

children.name: Myzomela jugularis
children.description: The sulphur-breasted myzomela, also known as the orange-breasted myzomela or orange-breasted honeyeater, is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae.

children.name: Myzomela lafargei
children.description: The red-capped myzomela, also known as the scarlet-naped myzomela, is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is found in the Solomon Islands archipelago. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Myzomela rosenbergii
children.description: The red-collared myzomela is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is found in New Guinea. The long-billed myzomela of Goodenough Island was formerly considered conspecific, but was split as a distinct species by the IOC in 2021.

children.name: Myzomela tristrami
children.description: The sooty myzomela is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to Makira.

children.name: Manorina
children.description: Manorina — a genus of honeyeaters in the family Meliphagidae.

children.name: Manorina melanocephala
children.description: The noisy miner is a bird in the honeyeater family, Meliphagidae, and is endemic to eastern and southeastern Australia. This miner is a grey bird, with a black head, orange-yellow beak and feet, a distinctive yellow patch behind the eye, and white tips on the tail feathers. The Tasmanian subspecies has a more intense yellow panel in the wing, and a broader white tip to the tail.

children.name: Manorina viridis
children.description: Manorina viridis — a species in the genus Manorina, family Meliphagidae. Also known as honeyeaters.
